title: Compaction v2: token-budgeted, structure-preserving context compression
current_state: History management is message-COUNT based (hard-truncate at 20, Groq-summarize at 30) — blind to actual token weight. One huge tool_result counts the same as 'ok'. Summaries flatten tool activity into prose, losing which files were touched. The T-148 keystone bug lived exactly here.
target_state: Budget by estimated tokens (len/4 heuristic, no tokenizer dep): keep recent turns verbatim within a configurable budget (default ~24k tokens for root); when over budget, oldest spans compress via the existing Groq path into a structured digest that PRESERVES: files read/edited (paths), tools called + one-line outcomes, decisions made, open questions. Tool_use/tool_result pairing never splits (extend safe-slice). The digest block carries a marker so test_conversation_golden can assert no in-window turn was dropped AND the digest names every compressed-away file-touch.
migration_plan: 1. agent/truncation.py: estimate_tokens(messages) helper; budget param threaded from ModeConfig (new field ctx_token_budget, None = legacy count behavior).
2. Structured digest prompt for compress_messages_with_groq: explicit sections (FILES, TOOLS, DECISIONS, OPEN).
3. Root ModeConfig gets ctx_token_budget=24000; normie keeps ctx_message_window (cheap tier, small anyway).
4. Gate test extension: golden conversation with a fat tool_result — assert budget respected, pairing intact, digest names the compressed file-touches.
5. CompressionFailed fallback stays (hard truncate guarantees progress).
risk_notes: This is the T-148 neighborhood — gate tests are the contract. Do AFTER C1 lands. RISK_FLAGGED → interactive.
